design_philosophy.core_loop: Side-scrolling beat 'em up: defeat enemies with melee weapons/magic, ride creatures (Bizarrians), progress through branching paths to confront final boss
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Combat crowd control balanced against resource management (magic potions from defeated enemies and gnomes)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Wield elemental magic and master a diverse roster of warriors (giant, panther humanoid, swordsmen/swordswoman) to reclaim the Golden Axe and rescue the princess
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Iterative design philosophy that regressed rather than innovated—restored mechanics from Golden Axe I (gnome magic item drops, mounting creatures) while removing Golden Axe II improvements (magic meter charging). Forced all-or-nothing magic usage. Conservative approach to sequel design that prioritized familiarity over advancement.

difficulty.lives_system: Extra lives earned by freeing prisoners scattered throughout levels (some in barrels, crystal cells, or guarded by enemies)
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based progression with junction points allowing player route selection
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High: players choose their own path through branching routes; route selection likely impacts difficulty progression

progression.upgrade_system: Character-specific special abilities (Proud: airslam throw; Chronos & Sarah: double jump, wall jump). Team attacks available in cooperative multiplayer. Character roster selection (5 playable warriors) determines move set and magic effects
progression.unlock_mechanism: Route junction system allows players to select paths to final encounter. High score requirement unlocks second form of final boss (Damud Hellstrike) and good ending
replayability_hooks: Four character routes with distinct movesets and magic abilities
Branching path selection creates multiple progressions through stages
VS mode with full character roster (including bosses) for one-on-one combat
Score-based unlock of enhanced final boss encounter and alternate ending
Single-player AI mode in VS for repeated competitive play

world_building_techniques: Curse removal quest framing: one hero has curse relieved by Gillius Thunderhead and must free other cursed warriors
Environmental transformation: king transformed into Griffin minion Eve; world corrupted by Damud Hellstrike
Character cameos: Gillius Thunderhead (original game protagonist) appears in cutscenes to drive narrative

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters serve as stage culminations with character-specific interaction patterns. Eve (transformed king) functions as mid-game/optional boss. Damud Hellstrike split into two phases based on player performance/score
notable_bosses: Damud Hellstrike (primary antagonist with unlockable second form based on score)

faction_indicators: Princess of Yuria (kidnapped by Damud—object of rescue)
Damud Hellstrike faction (Prince of Darkness, curses warriors, steals Golden Axe)
Gillius Thunderhead (dwarf ally, curse-breaker, represents original hero lineage)
danger_signals: Curse placed on all warriors (systemic threat to protagonist pool)
Golden Axe theft (loss of civilization's magical artifact)
King corruption into Griffin minion (authority structure compromised)
environmental_storytelling: Prisoner liberation mechanics signal oppression/occupation of stages
Bizarrian mounts as corrupted/mutated creatures available for ride
Magic potion distribution via gnomes indicates resource scarcity
Branching routes suggest fractured world/multiple resistance paths
